title: integrálás
text:
határozatlan integrál

anti-derivált, primitív függvény

\[
   \left( \int f\right)'=f
\]



tulajdonságok

\(C,D\in\mathbb{R}\)

\[\begin{gather*}
   \int\left( Cf+g\right) =C\int{f}+\int{g}\\
   \mint{C}{x} =  Cx+D\\
   \mint{x^{C}}{x} =  \frac{x^{C+1}}{C+1}+D\hspace{1cm} C\neq-1\\
   \int{\sin}=-\cos+C\\
   \int{\cos}=\sin+C\\
   \mint{\frac{1}{x}}{x}=\log{|x|}+C\hspace{1cm}x\neq0\\
   \mint{C^x}{x}=\frac{C^x}{\log{C}}+D\hspace{1cm}C>0,C\neq1\\
   \int\left(f'g+fg'\right)=fg+C\\
   \int f(g)g'=\left( \int f \right)(g)+C
\end{gather*}\]



számoljuk ki a következő integrálokat

\(\mint{xe^{x^2}}{x}\)

válasz: \[\begin{gather*}
\mder{e^{x^2}}=2xe^{x^2} \ \ \rightarrow \  \frac{e^{x^2}}{2}+C
\end{gather*}\]

\(\mint{\sin(x)e^x}{x}\)

válasz: \[\begin{gather*}
\text{(1)}\ \ \mint{\sin(x)e^{x}}{x}=-\cos(x)e^{x}-\mint{-\cos(x)e^{x}}{x}=\\
-\cos(x)e^{x}+\mint{\cos(x)e^{x}}{x}\\
\text{(2)}\ \ \mint{\sin(x)e^{x}}{x}=\sin(x)e^{x}-\mint{\cos(x)e^{x}}{x}\\
\text{(1)+(2)}\ \rightarrow \\
\mint{\sin(x)e^{x}}{x}=\frac{\sin(x)-\cos(x)}{2}e^{x}+C
\end{gather*}\]

\(\mint{\cos^3(x)}{x}\)

válasz: \[\begin{gather*}
\text{helyettesítés: }\ u=sin(x)\\
\mint{\cos^3(x)}{x}=\mint{(1-\sin^2(x))\cos(x)}{x}=\\
\mint{1-u^2}{u}=u-\frac{u^3}{3}+C=\sin(x)-\frac{\sin^3(x)}{3}+C
\end{gather*}\]

\(\mint{e^{au}\sin(u)}{u}\ \ 0\neq a\in\mathbb{R}\)

válasz: \[\begin{gather*}
\text{parciális integrálás: }\ f=e^{au},\ \ g'=\sin(u)\\
\mint{e^{au}\sin(u)}{u}=-e^{au}\cos(u)-\mint{ae^{au}(-\cos(u))}{u}=\\
=-e^{au}\cos(u)+a\mint{e^{au}\cos(u)}{u}\\
\text{parciális integrálás: }\ f'=e^{au},\ \ g=\sin(u)\\
\mint{e^{au}\sin(u)}{u}=\frac{e^{au}}{a}\sin(u)-\mint{\frac{e^{au}}{a}\cos(u)}{u}=\\
\frac{e^{au}}{a}\sin(u)-\frac{1}{a}\mint{e^{au}\cos(u)}{u}\\
\text{szorozzuk meg $a^2$-el a másodikat és adjuk össze az elsővel}\\
(1+a^2)\mint{e^{au}\sin(u)}{u}=ae^{au}\sin(u)-e^{au}\cos(u) \ \ \implies\\
\mint{e^{au}\sin(u)}{u}=\frac{e^{au}\left({a\sin(u)-\cos(u)}\right)}{1+a^2}
\end{gather*}\]

title: sillabusz
text:
Differenciálegyenletek bevezetése példák segítségével (szabadesés-, harmonikus rezgőmozgás-, rádióaktív bomlás egyenlete).
Differenciálegyenletek osztályozása, n-edrendű, implicit/explicit közönséges differenciálegyenletek (KDE), elsőrendű közönséges differenciálegyenletek megoldása.
Kezdeti érték feltétel, illetve -probléma. Vonalelem, iránymező definíciója.
Elemi úton megoldható elsőrendű közönséges differenciálegyenletek.
Homogén/inhomogén lineáris differenciálegyenletek és megoldásuk.
Egyenletrendszerek, magasabb rendű egyenletek, átviteli elv.
Állandó együtthatós, másodrendű, homogén, lineáris differenciálegyenlet és azok karakterisztikus egyenlete, megoldása.
Differenciálegyenletek numerikus megoldása (fokozatos közelítés, Euler-, Runge-Kutta módszerek).
Magasabb rendű egyenletek numerikus megoldása.
Függvények közelítése polinomokkal: hatványsor, Taylor-sor és konvergencia sugár.
Fourier sorok, együtthatók kiszámolása általános, illetve páros és páratlan függvények esetén.
Fourier transzformált és inverz Fourier transzformált és azok tulajdonságai.
Diszkrét/Inverz diszkrét Fourier transzformáció.
Laplace transzformáció definíciója, tulajdonságai, kiszámítása és alkalmazása differenciálegyenletek megoldásához.
A fent megnevezett témakörökhöz kapcsolódó MATLAB ismeretek elsajátítása (iránymezők kirajzoltatása, első- és magasabb rendű differenciálegyenletek megoldása, hatványsorok, Fourier-sorok és Fourier-transzformáltak segítségével kapott közelítő függvények kirajzoltatása, Laplace transzformáltak meghatározása).

title: norm/concepts_draft
text:
in this section \(x,y \in \mathbb{R}\)
how large is a real number?
absolute value, distance from zero

\[
\vert x \vert =
\begin{cases}
  x & \text{ if }\ \ x\ge 0\\
  -x & \text{ otherwise }
\end{cases}
\]

properties:

\(\vert x\vert\ge 0\) and \(\vert x\vert=0\ \text{ iff }\ x=0\)
\(\vert x+y \vert \le \vert x\vert + \vert y \vert\)
\(\vert x\cdot y \vert = \vert x\vert \cdot \vert y \vert\)

title: norm/concepts_draft
text:
in this section \(x,y \in \mathbb{R}\)
how large is a real number?
absolute value, distance from zero

\[
\vert x \vert =
\begin{cases}
  x & \text{ if }\ \ x\ge 0\\
  -x & \text{ otherwise }
\end{cases}
\]

properties:

\(\vert x\vert\ge 0\) and \(\vert x\vert=0\ \text{ iff }\ x=0\)
\(\vert x+y \vert \le \vert x\vert + \vert y \vert\)
\(\vert x\cdot y \vert = \vert x\vert \cdot \vert y \vert\)

title: norm/concepts_draft
text:
review

in this section \(x,y \in \mathbb{R}^n\) and \(\alpha \in \mathbb{R}\)
how large is a vector?
it is denoted by \(\Vert x \Vert\)
requirements (axioms):

\(\Vert x\Vert\ge 0\) and \(\Vert x\Vert=0\ \text{ iff }\ x=0\)
\(\Vert x+y \Vert \le \Vert x\Vert + \Vert y \Vert\)

it is the triangle inequality

\(\Vert \alpha \cdot x \Vert = \vert \alpha\vert \cdot \Vert x \Vert\)

the following vector norms are important

\(\Vert x \Vert_{2} = \sqrt{\vert x_1\vert^2 + \ldots + \vert x_n\vert^2}\ \ \) (euclidean or 2-norm)
\(\Vert x \Vert_{1} = \vert x_1\vert + \ldots + \vert x_n\vert\ \ \) (manhattan or 1-norm)
\(\Vert x \Vert_{\infty} = \max_{i=k\ldots n}\vert x_k\vert\ \ \) (\(\infty\)-norm)

the distance between vectors: \(\Vert x-y\Vert\)

small: close to each other
large: far from each other

read this for more details

title: norm/concepts_draft
text:
additional-properties

for all \(x \in \mathbb{R}^{n}\) \[
\Vert x\Vert_{\infty} \le \Vert x\Vert_{2} \le \Vert x\Vert_{1}
\]

title: norm/concepts_draft
text:
matlab

norm(x,1), norm(x,2), norm(x,inf)

title: norm/concepts_draft
text:
properties

the following properties are also valid

\[\begin{gather*}
\Vert E \Vert = 1\\
\Vert A\cdot x\Vert \le \Vert A\Vert \cdot \Vert x \Vert\\
\Vert A\cdot B \Vert \le \Vert A\Vert \cdot \Vert B \Vert\\
\end{gather*}\]

title: norm/concepts_draft
text:
additional properties

for all \(A \in {\cal M}_{n\times n}\): \[
\Vert A\Vert_{\infty} \le \Vert A\Vert_{2} \le \Vert A\Vert_{1}
\]

title: norm/concepts_draft
text:
computational rules

\(a_{ij}\) is \(j\)-th element of the \(i\)-th row for a matrix \(A\)


1-norm
\[
\Vert A\Vert _1=\max\limits_{1\leq j\leq n}\sum\limits_{i=1}^n\vert
a_{ij}\vert
\]

for each column compute the sum of absolute values and take the maximal one



2 norm
\[
\Vert A\Vert _2=\sqrt{\lambda _{\max} (A^{T}A)}
\]

take the square root of the maximal absolute value eigenvalue of the matrix \((A^{T}A)\)



\(\infty\) norm
\[
\Vert A\Vert _\infty=\max_{1\leq i\leq n}\sum_{j=1}^n\vert a_{ij}\vert
\]

for each row compute the sum of absolute values and take the maximal one

read this for details

title: norm/concepts_draft
text:
numerical-example
\[
A=\left[\begin{array}{rrr}
-3&0&4\\
1&-1&2\\
-2&1&-2
\end{array}\right]
\]

\(\Vert A\Vert_{1}= 8\)
\(\Vert A\Vert_{\infty}= 7\)

title: norm/concepts_draft
text:
matlab

norm(A,1), norm(A,2), norm(A,inf)

title: norm/concepts_draft
text:
example (linsys)

compare the solutions of the linear systems below \[
\left[
\begin{array}{rr}
1&1\\
1&1.0001
\end{array}
\right]
\left[\begin{array}{r}
x_1\\
x_2
\end{array}\right] =
\left[\begin{array}{r}
2\\
2
\end{array}\right]
\]

\(x=[2,0]^{T}\) \[
\left[
\begin{array}{rr}
1&1\\
1&1.0001
\end{array}
\right]
\left[\begin{array}{r}
y_1\\
y_2
\end{array}\right] =
\left[\begin{array}{r}
2\\
2.0001
\end{array}\right]
\]
\(y=[1,1]^{T}\)

large difference, despite the matrix is the same and the right hand sides are very close to each other (in vector norm!)
how can we explain this behavior?

title: norm/concepts_draft
text:
inequality

this is the error in the right hand side inequality
let the matrix \(A\) be invertible and fix a vector norm and the corresponding matrix norm
consider the linear systems: \[\begin{align*}
Ax &= b \\
A(x+\Delta{x}) &= b+\Delta{b}
\end{align*}\]
we’ll have different solutions for the systems, but how far they are from each other?
the following inequality holds: \[
\frac{\Vert \Delta{x}\Vert}{\Vert x\Vert} \le \Vert A\Vert \Vert A^{-1}\Vert \frac{\Vert \Delta{b}\Vert}{\Vert b\Vert}
\]
the inequality is tight, no gap between the sides
read it: the relative error in the solution is in the worst case is \(\Vert A\Vert \Vert A^{-1}\Vert\) times the relative error in the right hand side
the quantity \(\Vert A\Vert \Vert A^{-1}\Vert\) is called the conditional number of \(A\)

\(\text{cond}(A)=\Vert A\Vert \Vert A^{-1}\Vert\)

read this for more details \[
x_{\text{relative error}} \le \text{cond}(A) b_{\text{relative error}}
\]
important: this kind of error propagation has nothing to do with floating point computational errors.

title: norm/concepts_draft
text:
matlab

cond(A,1), cond(A,2), cond(A,inf)

title: norm/concepts_draft
norm/concepts_draft
text:
properties

its value does depend on the norm used
\(\text{cond}(A) \geq 1\)
for \(c\neq 0\), \(\text{cond}(cA)=\text{cond}(A)\)

read this for more details

title: norm/concepts_draft
text:
ill-conditioned

if \(\text{cond}(A)*b_{\text{relative error}}\ge 1\) the system is called ill-conditioned
it means that the absolute error in the solution can be as large as the true solution itself - it is bad.

title: binary expansion of integers
text:
\(x\) is a positive integer
the first expansion exists and unique

\[\begin{align}
x &= 2^{n}x_{n}+2^{n-1}x_{n-1}+\ldots+2^{1}x_{1}+2^{0}x_{0} \ \ x_{i}\in \{0,1\} \\  
x &= 2q+r \\
x &= 2(2^{n-1}x_{n}+2^{n-2}x_{n-1}+\ldots+x_{1})+x_{0}
\end{align}\]

title: binary expansion of fractions
text:
\(x \in [0,1)\)
the first expansion exists and unique, assuming that the tail of \(x_{n}\) is not a constant 1 sequence.
\(2x - x_{1}\) will be the “new” \(x\)

\[\begin{align}
x &= 2^{-1}x_{1}+2^{-2}x_{2}+\ldots+2^{-n}x_{n}+\ldots \\
2x &= x_{1}+ 2^{-1}x_{2}+\ldots+2^{n+1}x_{n}+\ldots \\
2x - x_{1} &= 2^{-1}x_{2}+\ldots+2^{n+1}x_{n}+\ldots
\end{align}\]

title: syllabus
text:
Topics

Floating point systems (properties, rounding, errors)
Systems of linear equation with error in the right-hand side, matrix norms, condition number.
Numerical solution of linear systems (LU,PLU,Cholesky)
Least square approximation.
Polynomial interpolation (Lagrange, Hermite, spline)
Numerical integration.
Numerical methods for solving of nonlinear equations and systems.
Optimization



Requirements for the practice grade

At most 3 absences from the labors.
There will a grading test at the end of the study period, and it will consist both numerical/multiple choice/matlab questions.
The grade will be determined by the following evaluation system:

0% - 50% \(\longrightarrow\) 1
50% - 70% \(\longrightarrow\) 2
70% - 85% \(\longrightarrow\) 3
85% - 95% \(\longrightarrow\) 4
95% -100% \(\longrightarrow\) 5

The exact date of the test will be announced on the e-learning site.
The retake will be held in the exam period.
The sample test collections on the e-learning site and the shared exercises on this site are serving as the basis for the grading test.
In the study period at the time of lectures there will be some extra tests. The points obtained by these tests can be used to increase your grading test’s result.

title: About
text: Some education related pages.

title: syllabus
text:
Topics

Floating point systems (properties, rounding, errors)
Systems of linear equation with error in the right-hand side, matrix norms, condition number.
Numerical solution of linear systems (LU,PLU,Cholesky)
Least square approximation.
Polynomial interpolation (Lagrange, Hermite, spline)
Numerical integration.
Eigenvalue problems, sparse systems.
Numerical solution of nonlinear equations and systems, minimization.
Linear programming (graphical and 1,2-phase simplex method) Duality in linear programming, sensitivity analysis. Algorithms for solving transportation and assignment problems.



Requirements for the practice grade

At most 3 absences from labors. At most 3 absences from practices. Note that the practice is mandatory.
There will a grading test at the end of the study period, and it will consist both numerical/multiple choice/matlab questions.
The grade will be determined by the following evaluation system:

0% - 50% \(\longrightarrow\) 1
50% - 70% \(\longrightarrow\) 2
70% - 85% \(\longrightarrow\) 3
85% - 95% \(\longrightarrow\) 4
95% -100% \(\longrightarrow\) 5

The exact date of the test will be announced on the e-learning site.
The retake will be held in the exam period.
The sample test collections on the e-learning site and the shared exercises on this site are serving as the basis for the grading test.
In the study period at the time of practices there will be some extra tests. The points obtained by these tests can be used to increase your grading test’s result.

by removing the even indexed elements

b=b(floor(tmp)==tmp);

whose main diagonal (downward) is \([3,4,\ldots,101,102]\) and every other elements are zero

title: norm/notes_norm
text:
\(\Vert x+y\Vert_{2} \le \Vert x\Vert_{2}+\Vert y\Vert_{2}\)
inner/scalar/dot product: \(\langle x,y\rangle=\sum_{i=1}^{n} x_{k}y_{k}\)
\(\Vert x\Vert_{2}=\sqrt{\langle x,y\rangle}\)
an important inequality related to the scalar product: CBS-inequality
the triangle inequality for \(\Vert . \Vert_{2}\) is the consequence of the CBS-inequality
equality holds iff. \(x=0 \ \text{ or }\  y=0 \ \text{ or }\  x=\lambda y \text{ for some } \lambda>0\)
the two inequalities in \(\mathbb{R}^{n}\) are the same (equivalent)

title: norm/notes_norm
text:
\(\infty\)-norm
\[\begin{gather*}
\vert(Ax)_{i}\vert=
\vert\sum_{j} a_{ij} x_{j}\vert\le
\sum_{j} \vert a_{ij}\vert \vert x_{j}\vert\le
\sum_{j} \vert a_{ij}\vert \Vert x\Vert_{\infty}\le
\Vert A\Vert_{\infty} \vert \Vert x\Vert_{\infty}
\end{gather*}\] if the maximum is attained in the \(i\)-th row then for the sign vector of that row equality holds.

title: norm/notes_norm
text:
\(1\)-norm
\[\begin{gather*}
\sum_{i}\vert(Ax)_{i}\vert\le
\sum_{i}\sum_{j} \vert a_{ij}\vert \vert x_{j}\vert=
\sum_{j} \vert x_{j}\vert \sum_{i} \vert a_{ij}\vert \le
\sum_{j} \vert x_{j}\vert \Vert A\Vert_{1} \le
\Vert x\Vert_{1} \Vert A\Vert_{1}
\end{gather*}\] if the maximum is attained in the \(j\)-th column then for the vector \(e_{j}\) the two sides are equal.

title: norm/notes_norm
text:
special vectors
the computations above provide a way to find a vector \(x\) for which \(\Vert Ax\Vert=\Vert A\Vert\Vert x\Vert\) in the cases of \(1\) and \(\infty\) norms.

title: vizsga
text:
projektek

beadás az e-learning felületen keresztül
a formátum itt lesz meghatározva, később

online teszt

az e-learning felületen, moodle-teszt formában

title: elsőrendű lineáris homogén
text:
alakja
\[
   y'+A(t)y=0
\]


megoldása
\[
   y=Ce^{-\mint{A(t)}{t}} \hspace{1cm} C\in\mathbb{R}
\]

Speciálisan, ha \(A(t)=A\) konstans \[
y=Ce^{-At} \hspace{1cm} C\in\mathbb{R}
\]

title: differenciálás
text:
pillanatnyi változási gyorsaság

az érintő \(x\) tengellyel bezárt szögének tangense, meredekség.

\[
   f'(x_0)=\lim_{h\to 0} \frac{f(x_0+h)-f(x_0)}{h}
\]



deriválás és műveletek (

legyen \(C\in\mathbb{R}\)

\[\begin{gather*}
  (Cf)'=cf'\hspace{1cm}\\
  (f+g)=f'+g'\\
  (fg)'=f'g+fg'\\
  \left( \frac{f}{g}\right) '=\frac{f'g-fg'}{g^2}\hspace{1cm} g\neq 0\\
  f(g)'=f'(g)g'\\
  (f^{-1})'(x)=\frac{1}{f'(f^{-1}(x))}
\end{gather*}\]



elemi függvények deriváltjai
\[\begin{gather*}
  C' =  0\\
  (x^{C})' = Cx^{C-1}\\
  \exp(x)'=exp(x)\\
  (C^x)'=\log(C)C^x\hspace{1cm}C>0\\
  \sin(x)'=\cos(x)\\
  \cos(x)'=-\sin(x)\\
  \tan(x)'=\frac{1}{\cos(x)^2}\\
  \cot(x)'=-\frac{1}{\sin(x)^2}\\
  \log(|x|)'=\frac{1}{x}\hspace{1cm}x\neq0
\end{gather*}\]



számoljuk ki \(f'\)-t adott \(f\) esetén

\(f(x)=e^{xe^{\sin(x)}}\)

válasz: \(e^{xe^{\sin(x)}}(e^{\sin(x)}+xe^{\sin(x)}\cos(x))\)

\(f(x)=\frac{\log(x\log(x))}{x^2}\)

válasz: \(\frac{\log(x) + 1}{x^3\log(x)} - \frac{2\log(x\log(x))}{x^3}\)





matlab

syms, diff

title: linsys/concepts_draft
text:
setting up an appropriate SLE is the hardest part of the problem solving…but here we only deal with the “how to solve it” part.
our aim is to solve systems of linear equations (SLE’s)
we have \(m\) equations \(n\) variables, a cofficient matrix \(A\) and a right hand side \(b\) \[
\begin{gather*}
a_{11}x_{1}+\ldots+a_{1n}x_{n} = b_{1}\\
a_{21}x_{1}+\ldots+a_{2n}x_{n} = b_{2}\\
\vdots{}\\
a_{m1}x_{1}+\ldots+a_{mn}x_{n} = b_{m}\\
\text{or}\\
Ax=b\\
\text{or}\\
x_{1}a_{.1}+x_{2}a_{.2}+\ldots+x_{n}a_{.n}=b\\
\end{gather*}
\]
here by \(a_{.j}\) we mean the \(j\)-th column of \(A\)
the third form is the most expressive one

it tells us that we are working in vector spaces
it tells us that we are searching for a linear combination of the column vectors that is \(b\) and the solution for the system (if any) is coefficiant set of the combination.
from linear algebra we know that we have three cases:

we cannot express \(b\) by means of the given system (0 solutions)
we can express \(b\) uniquely (1-solution)
we can express \(b\) in several different ways (\(\infty\)-solutions)

title: linsys/concepts_draft
text:
the aim is to reduce the original system to a simpler but equivalent one - which can be solved trivially
we denote the \(i\)-th row by \(a_{i.}\)
these are the equivalent (they keep the solution system intact) transformations of a LSE:

multiply the \(i\)-th row by a nonzero scalar: \(a_{i.}=\lambda a_{i.}, \ \ b_{i}=\lambda b_{i}\)
elimination: \(a_{j.}=a_{j.}-\lambda a_{i.},\ \ b_{j}=b_{j}-\lambda b_{i}\)
swap the \(i\)-th and \(j\)-th row: \(a_{i.}\leftrightarrow a_{j.},\ b_{i}\leftrightarrow b_{j}\)

each of the transformation above can be described as a matrix operation (\(M_{k}\)): \[
\begin{gather*}
Ax=b\ \rightarrow \\
M_{1}Ax=M_{1}b\ \rightarrow \\
M_{2}M_{1}Ax=M_{2}M_{1}b \ \rightarrow \\
\ldots \\
M_{K}\ldots M_{2}M_{1}Ax=M_{K}\ldots M_{2}M_{1}b
\end{gather*}
\]
where each of the \(M_{k}\)’s are invertible \(m\times m\) matrices. That’s why the set of solutions does not change.
at the end of these steps (hopefully) we will have a simpler system.

title: linsys/concepts_draft
text:
\(0\) solutions
>> A=[1,2,3;3,2,1;2,1,3;-2,0,2]; b=[1;2;3;1];
>> ge=mkGauss([A,b]);
       1              2              3              1       
       3              2              1              2       
       2              1              3              3       
      -2              0              2              1       

>> ge.subtract(3,1,2);
       1              2              3              1       
       0             -4             -8             -1       
       2              1              3              3       
      -2              0              2              1       

>> ge.subtract(2,1,3);
       1              2              3              1       
       0             -4             -8             -1       
       0             -3             -3              1       
      -2              0              2              1       

>> ge.subtract(-2,1,4);
       1              2              3              1       
       0             -4             -8             -1       
       0             -3             -3              1       
       0              4              8              3       

>> ge.subtract(3/4,2,3);
       1              2              3              1       
       0             -4             -8             -1       
       0              0              3              7/4     
       0              4              8              3       

>> ge.subtract(-1,2,4);
       1              2              3              1       
       0             -4             -8             -1       
       0              0              3              7/4     
       0              0              0              2       

here we stopped after seeing the upper-triangle form, for it is clear that we have no solution.

title: linsys/concepts_draft
text:
matlab

the function rref can and should be used. it also performs the backward elimination steps and the rows are normalized.

title: linsys/concepts_draft
text:
\(\infty\) solutions
>> format rat;
>> A=[1,2,3,4;3,4,2,1;4,2,1,3]; b=[1;2;4];
>> rref([A,b])

ans =

       1              0              0              1              6/5     
       0              1              0             -3/2          -11/20    
       0              0              1              2              3/10    

the parametric solution system is the following \[
x=\left[\begin{array}{c}
\frac{6}{5}\\
-\frac{11}{20}\\
\frac{3}{10}\\
0\\
\end{array}\right]-
\lambda\left[\begin{array}{c}
1\\
-\frac{3}{2}\\
2\\
-1\\
\end{array}\right]
\]
we often have some restrictions for the solution, for example we need a solution with positive components. for this example we can easily fulfill this restriction.
this parametric solution can also be obtained by calling null(A,"rational").

title: linsys/concepts_draft
text:
example

for this computation this program is used.

>> A=[4,1,-1;-1,8,0;1,1,4];
>> [L,U]=mylu(A)

phase 0:

   1   0   0   4   1  -1
   0   1   0  -1   8   0
   0   0   1   1   1   4

press a key to continue


phase 1:

  sub-phase 2:

    subtract -1/4 times the 1. row from the 2. row

     1     0     0     4     1    -1
  -1/4     1     0     0  33/4  -1/4
     0     0     1     1     1     4

press a key to continue


  sub-phase 3:

    subtract 1/4 times the 1. row from the 3. row

     1     0     0     4     1    -1
  -1/4     1     0     0  33/4  -1/4
   1/4     0     1     0   3/4  17/4

press a key to continue


phase 2:

  sub-phase 3:

    subtract 1/11 times the 2. row from the 3. row

      1      0      0      4      1     -1
   -1/4      1      0      0   33/4   -1/4
    1/4   1/11      1      0      0  47/11

press a key to continue


L =

       1              0              0       
      -1/4            1              0       
       1/4            1/11           1       


U =

       4              1             -1       
       0             33/4           -1/4     
       0              0             47/11

title: linsys/concepts_draft
text:
matlab

the function call [L,U,P]=lu(A) will create a \(PLU\) factorization for the parameter matrix. Almost always row-swaps will be performed.
the backslash (\) operator is a built-in for solving linear systems.

>> A=[4,1,-1;-1,8,0;1,1,4];b=[1;2;4];
>> x=A\b;
>> x

x =

    0.3830
    0.2979
    0.8298

>> A*x

ans =

    1.0000
    2.0000
    4.0000

PLU factorization

for an arbitrary square matrix \(A\) it is always exist. The used row swaps are represented by the matrix \(P\), which is a permutation matrix: it is obtained by reordering the rows/columns of the identity matrix.

title: linsys/concepts_draft
text:
determinant

assuming that no row multiplications were used, just multiply together the diagonal elements of \(U\) and take its negative if odd number of row swaps were used.

title: linsys/concepts_draft
text:
rank

that is: the dimension of the subspace generated by the column-vectors of \(A\)
count the number of non-zero elements in the main diagonal of \(U\)
